programme.title: Graveyard Carz
programme.desc: A 1970 GTX 440, in Vitamin C orange, is making headway through the paint shop; when it arrives in assembly, Mark puts Doug in charge of the forklift as he attempts to install a very big engine into a newly painted engine compartment.

programme.title: Garage Squad
programme.desc: Restoring a Split Window Corvette runs into unexpected problems; the owner of a '63 Corvette doesn't have time or money to keep it running.

programme.title: My Classic Car
programme.desc: My Classic Car captures America's love affair with the automobile! Dennis Gage, the handlebar mustachioed host, takes you along for the ride and keeps it entertaining with his unique perspective and enthusiasm for all things automotive.

programme.title: Orange County Choppers Original
programme.desc: Paul Sr. and the OCC crew head over to the University of South Florida to get schooled on the effects of Alzheimer's, and possibly get his head checked. They also spend an evening over at the OCC Roadhouse competing for top dog at Bandingo's trivia night.

programme.title: Dallas Car Sharks
programme.desc: A sporty 1974 Datsun 240Z gets the sharks circling around for the kill; a Jeep Wrangler brings Frank and JD toe-to-toe; Ash finds a way to bid at two different auctions at the same time.

programme.title: Dallas Car Sharks
programme.desc: A '59 Daimler Ferret military tank; Frank and JD battle it out for an '87 Jeep Wrangler that's in need of a lot of body work; Ash has his sights on a hoodless 2003 Mercedes SL500.

programme.title: Dallas Car Sharks
programme.desc: A Porsche 356 speedster replica comes up for auction; Ash's plan backfires when he tries to get the others to overpay for a vintage RV.

programme.title: Dallas Car Sharks
programme.desc: Four sharks compete against one another for a 1993 Ford Bronco; a 1938 Plymouth coupe fetches the day's highest bid.

programme.title: FantomWorks
programme.desc: Bob has spent years working on his beloved 1969 K20 Truck, but he needs FantomWorks to solve the mysteries within; Dan's brother-in-law, John, shows up with a 1962 Ferrari kit car that looks hot, but the wiring could burn it to the ground.

programme.title: FantomWorks
programme.desc: Kim and Doug want to drive her father's hyper-rare 1959 Thunderbolt, but the car was only set up for the racetrack; Batalla is a legendary motorcycle builder who asks Dan to restore his prized possession, a 1947 Knucklehead.

programme.title: Diesel Brothers
programme.desc: Diesel Dave picks his first giveaway truck and dreams big, attempting to build the 2020 version of the Kodiak; Todd LeDuc challenges the guys to a desert race and flips the script by picking The Muscle to be on his team.

programme.title: Diesel Brothers
programme.desc: Diesel Dave continues working on his epic modern-day Kodiak while making sure he doesn't break the bank; Sam the Mad Scientist unveils The Kraken; a diesel-powered monowheel vehicle that is unlike anything the guys have ever seen.

programme.title: Diesel Brothers
programme.desc: Mad Scientist Sam's wild creation, the Kraken, comes to life, but Heavy D continues to be skeptical of the build's potential viral success; a high-profile client wants to customize a Ram 3500, which puts Diesel Dave and The Muscle to the test.

programme.title: Diesel Brothers
programme.desc: Heavy D surprises the mechanics with his build: a tank; he has big plans for the beast, but must first find a new engine and get it moving again; Heavy D, Diesel Dave and the Muscle personally trick out their latest giveaway truck.

programme.title: Truck Dynasty
programme.desc: When Jerry Jr. learns that legendary chef Guy Fieri has bought a used Apocalypse Hellfire truck at an auction, he offers Guy free customization; Frank locks in a deal to build a custom Overland truck for internet superstar Nick The Wrangler Bishop.

programme.title: Truck Dynasty
programme.desc: A repeat client, Kim, calls as the company is about to leave their old shop; he wants an off-roading truck to comfortably seat his 11-person family, so Joe must get creative; Ashley's family revolts when Joe changes how sales calls are handled.

programme.title: Truck Dynasty
programme.sub-title: It's a Boat. It's a Truck. It's BoatTruck.
programme.desc: Due to a windfall from a custom project, Joe plans to create the Boat Truck, an amphibious Jeep; with a buyer for the Defender falling through, Pop, Jerry Jr. and Frank are tasked with finding buyers for both the Defender and the Boat Truck.

programme.title: Truck Dynasty
programme.desc: A repeat client wants a tall, big work truck, so the team decides to build the world's first-ever, lifted Hummer EV; Ashley sets a company sales goal of 25 trucks at the upcoming Palm Beach Boat Show, sparking family tension.

programme.title: Truck Dynasty
programme.desc: NBA star Shaquille O'Neal shows up unannounced at Apocalypse, to Jerry Jr.'s delight - he's been trying to get Shaq's attention for over a year; Joe begins work on Shaq's Juggernaut, but Jerry repeatedly interferes, forcing Ashley to step in.

programme.title: Truck Dynasty
programme.desc: Pop's returning customer from Texas wants a Ford Bronco converted into a pickup truck that can tow a 7,000-pound boat and is still fit for off-roading; Jerry Jr. orders the shop to paint a truck in a vivid neon green to chase likes and shares online.

programme.desc: The guys try to fix the 350-powered '56 Bel Air Field Car and road trip it to the surfside Samoa Drag Strip in California; an electrical issue the guys had never seen before turns what would have been beachside bliss into total disaster.

programme.title: Boy Meets World
programme.desc: A friend misinterprets Cory's act of Christmas kindness as charity.

programme.title: Boy Meets World
programme.desc: Cory and Eric invent some white lies in order to skip the annual father-son softball game.

programme.title: Boy Meets World
programme.desc: Cory and Eric decide to play detective when they discover provocative clothing in their mother's bowling bag.

programme.title: Boy Meets World
programme.sub-title: She Loves Me, She Loves Me Not
programme.desc: Cory is perplexed when he learns that his brother Eric is the object of Topanga's affection.

programme.title: The 700 Club
programme.desc: Meet ordinary people who have experienced the extraordinary power of prayer.

programme.title: 101 Dalmatians
programme.desc: Fashion designer Anita and computer-game writer Roger meet, fall in love and marry along with their Dalmatians Perdita and Pongo. The dogs' puppies are kidnapped by Anita's boss Cruella De Vil, who is stealing young Dalmatians to make the coat she has set her heart on. Pongo and Perdita set out to find and rescue all ninety-nine pups from their fearsome captors.

programme.title: Maleficent
programme.desc: As a beautiful young woman of pure heart, Maleficent (Angelina Jolie) has an idyllic life in a forest kingdom. When an invading army threatens the land, Maleficent rises up to become its fiercest protector. However, a terrible betrayal hardens her heart and twists her into a creature bent on revenge. She engages in an epic battle with the invading king's successor, then curses his newborn daughter, Aurora -- realizing only later that the child holds the key to peace in the kingdom.

programme.title: Maleficent: Mistress of Evil
programme.desc: Maleficent travels to a grand old castle to celebrate young Aurora's upcoming wedding to Prince Phillip. While there, she meets Aurora's future mother-in-law -- a conniving queen who hatches a devious plot to destroy the land's fairies. Hoping to stop her, Maleficent joins forces with a seasoned warrior and a group of outcasts to battle the queen and her powerful army.

programme.title: Cruella
programme.desc: Estella is a young and clever grifter who's determined to make a name for herself in the fashion world. She soon meets a pair of thieves who appreciate her appetite for mischief, and together they build a life for themselves on the streets of London. However, when Estella befriends fashion legend Baroness von Hellman, she embraces her wicked side to become the raucous and revenge-bent Cruella.

programme.title: Lyle, Lyle, Crocodile
programme.desc: When the Primm family moves to New York City, their young son, Josh, struggles to adapt to his new school and friends. All of that changes when he discovers Lyle, a singing crocodile that loves baths, caviar and great music. The two become fast friends, but when evil neighbor Mr. Grumps threatens Lyle's existence, the Primms must band together to show the world that family can come from the most unexpected places.

programme.title: Coco
programme.desc: Despite his family's generations-old ban on music, young Miguel dreams of becoming an accomplished musician like his idol Ernesto de la Cruz. Desperate to prove his talent, Miguel finds himself in the stunning and colorful Land of the Dead. After meeting a charming trickster named Héctor, the two new friends embark on an extraordinary journey to unlock the real story behind Miguel's family history.

programme.title: The Incredibles
programme.desc: In this lauded Pixar animated film, married superheroes Mr. Incredible (Craig T. Nelson) and Elastigirl (Holly Hunter) are forced to assume mundane lives as Bob and Helen Parr after all super-powered activities have been banned by the government. While Mr. Incredible loves his wife and kids, he longs to return to a life of adventure, and he gets a chance when summoned to an island to battle an out-of-control robot. Soon, Mr. Incredible is in trouble, and it's up to his family to save him.

programme.title: Incredibles 2
programme.desc: Telecommunications guru Winston Deavor enlists Elastigirl to fight crime and make the public fall in love with superheroes once again. That leaves Mr. Incredible with one of his greatest challenges ever -- staying home and taking care of three rambunctious children. As Violet, Dash and Jack-Jack offer him a new set of headaches, a cybercriminal named Screenslaver launches his dastardly plan -- hypnotizing the world through computer screens.

programme.title: Meet the Robinsons
programme.desc: Boy genius Lewis gives up hope of retrieving his latest invention, which was stolen by Bowler Hat Guy, then a young time-traveler named Wilbur Robinson arrives on the scene to whisk Lewis away in his time machine. The boys spend a day in the future with Wilbur's eccentric family and uncover an amazing secret at the same time.

programme.title: Small Town Hunting
programme.desc: Early season in Ohio can bring hot temps and late deer movement just before dark, but it can also be some of the best hunting of the season.

programme.title: Superfly
programme.desc: Cocaine kingpin Youngblood Priest realizes that it's time to get out of the game after surviving a violent attack from a crazed rival. Hoping for one last score, Priest and his partner travel to Mexico to arrange a deal. The career criminal now finds himself trying to outmaneuver the cartel, two corrupt police officers and all the double-crossers that threaten his path to freedom.

programme.title: Gemini Man
programme.desc: Henry Brogan is an elite 51-year-old assassin who's ready to call it quits after completing his 72nd job. His plans get turned upside down when he becomes the target of a mysterious operative who can seemingly predict his every move. To his horror, Brogan soon learns that the man who's trying to kill him is a younger, faster, cloned version of himself.

programme.title: Shadow People
programme.desc: A radio talk show host unravels a conspiracy about encounters with mysterious beings and their role in the unexplained deaths of several hundred victims in the 1980s.

programme.title: Shaft
programme.desc: Crooked cops on the take -- small-time drug lords -- sleazy informers and sadistic rich kids ready to kill --- for police detective John Shaft, its just another night in the underbelly of New York City, another shift facing down cops and criminals who want him dead and a legal system that thrives on money, not justice.

programme.title: The Manchurian Candidate
programme.desc: Years after his squad was ambushed during the Gulf War, Major Ben Marco (Denzel Washington) finds himself having terrible nightmares. He begins to doubt that his fellow squad-mate Sergeant Raymond Shaw (Liev Schreiber), now a vice-presidential candidate, is the hero he remembers him being. As Marco's doubts deepen, Shaw's political power grows, and, when Marco finds a mysterious implant embedded in his back, the memory of what really happened begins to return.

programme.title: Trouble Man
programme.desc: A former cop turned private investigator gets hired to find a missing R&B star. His investigation uncovers a connection to a larger conspiracy, forcing him to question those around him and his own past.

programme.title: Jurassic Hunt
programme.desc: A ragtag group of mercenaries and soldiers arrives at a mysterious reserve to hunt the deadliest game of all.

programme.title: Played
programme.desc: Rival gangs hire a British thug (Mick Rossi), who was imprisoned for eight years, to complete a hit in Los Angeles.

programme.title: Double Whammy
programme.desc: Ray Pluto (Denis Leary) has many problems. He is satirized in the tabloids as the "loser cop." His partner (Steve Buscemi) is starting to seem suspiciously attracted to him. A pair of screenwriters (Donald Faison, Keith Nobbs) across the hall keep bugging him for help. The superintendent of his building (Luis Guzmán) is stabbed by hoodlums hired by his own rebellious daughter (Melonie Diaz). To top it off, a sexually aggressive chiropractor (Elizabeth Hurley) may just be Ray's undoing.

programme.title: Civil Brand
programme.desc: Things are tough at Whitehead Correctional Institute, a notorious women's prison. The inmates include Lil' Mamma (Lark Voorhies), a pregnant teen, and Nikki (N'Bushe Wright), the queen bee of Whitehead. The inmates are under the supervision of abusive warden Dease (Clifton Powell), who forces them to endure hours of hard labor, from which he profits. Eventually, the women band together and reach out to part-time prison guard Michael (Mos Def), a law student, who helps them fight for reform.

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Fact or Fiction?
programme.desc: John McLane (Bruce Willis) finds himself dangling from the side of a building and must shoot through the glass window to break through to safety; reconstructing the scene using the same Beretta 92 handgun and office glass.

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Fact or Fiction?
programme.desc: John Rambo's ability to decimate enemies all by himself has created some of the most incredible weapon stunts in Hollywood history; testing the reality surrounding these iconic scenes from this blockbuster franchise.

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Fact or Fiction?
programme.desc: Can you really fire a GE Minigun handheld with ammunition held in a backpack (also seen on Captain America: Winter Soldier)? Additionally, for one of our larger tests - can you actually chop down a jungle forest with a minigun?

programme.title: Hollywood Weapons: Fact or Fiction?
programme.desc: Using a unique method to crack a safe, Nick Wells (Robert De Niro) uses a blowtorch to cut through a safe, fill it with water, and blow the door off using a small explosive device; the show tests this, along with the effects of a 20mm antitank rifle.

programme.title: The Re-Inventors
programme.desc: Rebuilding a 700-year-old multi-stage weapon brings multiple problems to Matt and Jeremy.

programme.title: Strange World
programme.desc: Alex searches for a scientific explanation for the terrifying phenomenon known as ice bombs, which cause fear, devastation and even injury.

programme.title: Strange World
programme.desc: Mysterious, alien sky sounds terrify Canadian residents, who struggle to locate their source.

programme.title: Strange World
programme.desc: Alex Hannaford heads to the US to investigate a `bird apocalypse', where it rained blackbirds.

programme.title: Strange World
programme.desc: Alex Hannaford heads to South Australia to investigate a terrifying rodent infestation.

programme.title: How to Build a City
programme.desc: The hidden stories of urban planning and infrastructure behind major cities are revealed; exploring how these factors have changed to suit the shifting population of the cities over time.

programme.title: How to Build a City
programme.desc: Innovation underscores this episode, set in one of the fastest growing cities in America. Ever on the cusp of discovering the next big thing, Seattle is where Amazon, Microsoft, Google are forging the future.

programme.title: How to Build a City
programme.desc: Explore how Berlin suffered from an image problem for decades; but the city at the epicentre of World War II is steadily gaining notoriety for all the right reasons; see how it's the leading start-up city in Europe -- uber-cool and cosmopolitan.

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.desc: Stormchaser George Kourounis visits Oklahoma City and chases a hail storm.

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.desc: George Kourounis joins forces with R.J. Evans to track huge lightning storms in the American southwest.

programme.title: Angry Planet
programme.desc: Heading to the Congo, George Kourounis climbs the steep and active Nyiragongo volcano.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded
programme.desc: When water unleashes its rage upon the planet, it becomes the ultimate bringer of death; whether it's from rain washing over the ground, rivers bursting their banks, or failed efforts to stem the flow, flooding is as inevitable as it is unstoppable.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded
programme.desc: Bridges and tunnels revolutionized transportation-but when they fail, tragedy follows. This episode explores the world's worst collapses and fires, including a tunnel blaze in the Alps so intense it fused 20 cars together. While bridge failures send vehicles plummeting into water or ground below, tunnel fires turn confined spaces into infernos. Why do these disasters happen, and what can be done to stop them? As we build bigger and faster, we also raise the stakes.

programme.title: Heavy Lifting
programme.desc: Maxum Crane replaces a grain handler at the port, but even the best laid plans go awry.

programme.title: Heavy Lifting
programme.desc: Dave does double duty, starting with work at a local college followed by maintenance on a historic amusement park ride.

programme.title: Heavy Lifting
programme.desc: Brad and Nathan Moore are called in to help build a new firefighter training unit.

programme.title: Secrets of the Factories
programme.desc: The Kellogg's factory in Manchester opens its doors to reveal step by step how some of the nation's favorite breakfast cereals are made in the biggest cereals factory in Europe, and the largest producer of cornflakes in the world.

programme.title: Made in Britain
programme.desc: Lord John Prescott visits the factories across Britain that makes some of the nation's favorite food and drinks.

programme.desc: It happened once and it took out the dinosaurs, it's only a matter of time before it happens again; the next time another space rock jeopardizes Earth, private space companies and their spacecrafts may not be enough to save the day.

programme.title: War Machines

programme.title: The Military Tech Show
programme.sub-title: NATO Maritime Centre, No Shipping-No Shopping
programme.desc: Visiting NATO's Centre for Maritime Research and Experimentation (CMRE) in La Spezia, Italy; the oceans are one of the last places that secret military tech can be kept hidden; solutions to keeping the world's shipping lanes safe for global trade.

programme.title: The Military Tech Show
programme.sub-title: Terrorist Threats, Sea Wasp Robot, Combat Medics
programme.desc: Swedish manufacturer SAAB's Sea Wasp, an under-water robot for detecting explosive devices; the iXblue Phins, an inertial system powered by fibre-optic gyroscopes aids underwater navigation; a life-like body made of silicon for combat medic training.

programme.title: The Military Tech Show
programme.sub-title: Warrior Vehicles, British Special Forces, Russian T14 Armata Tank
programme.desc: An Italian textile manufacturer uses a new form of wool for British special forces outfits.

programme.desc: The greatest hideout in fascist Italy's history -- the Monte Soratte bunker; it is built directly into a mountainside with security features even a Bond villain could only dream of.

programme.title: Dictators' Hideouts
programme.desc: Paranoid and isolated, Albanian dictator Enver Hoxha establishes his bunkerization project, which would result in the construction of more than 170,000 bunkers.

programme.title: Dictators' Hideouts
programme.desc: Infamous for the bunkers constructed during World War II and throughout the Cold War, Stalin's hideouts have maintained a shroud of secrecy for decades and are now seen for the first time.

programme.title: Ancient Engineering
programme.desc: Discovering the transport networks of the past.

programme.title: Ancient Engineering
programme.desc: Examining the history of the Great Wall of China.

programme.title: Ancient Engineering
programme.desc: Exploring how ancient Egyptian engineers built the monumental Great Pyramid at Giza, the only survivor of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World.

programme.title: Ancient Engineering
programme.desc: In the Middle Ages, the technology of the castle evolved by leaps and bounds.

programme.title: Ancient Engineering
programme.desc: Building for God reached a high point in the Middle Ages when a new engineering movement emerged in Europe.

programme.title: Ancient Engineering
programme.desc: Temples, designed to honor the Gods, have been at the heart of cities since earliest times; often they have been the biggest and most impressive buildings of their age.

programme.title: Ancient Engineering
programme.desc: Without water, civilization is simply not possible; Romans took control of their water supplies very, very seriously; in order to keep the taps on, Roman engineers constructed vast networks of aqueducts, tunnels, sewers, and drains.

programme.title: Ancient Engineering
programme.desc: As far back as the Bronze Age, humans were building ships capable of crossing oceans and trading between continents; perhaps the greatest surviving vessel from the Ancient world, is The Mary Rose, Henry VIII's mighty warship.

programme.title: Ancient Engineering
programme.desc: Even before the start of recorded history, ancient engineers across the World were erecting giant monuments.

programme.title: Ancient Engineering

programme.title: Battle Factory
programme.sub-title: Camouflage Makeup, Machine Gun and Amphibious Military
programme.desc: See how camouflage make-up, the M2 machine gun and the Argo amphibious ATV are built to withstand the toughest conditions.

programme.title: Battle Factory

programme.desc: Get to know the essential military kit that could mean the difference between life and death, including combat boots, the CZ 75 pistol, unmanned aerial vehicles and the rifle scope.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Members of the Robertson family recall their favorite memories from the show and share how the popular series impacted their lives.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Willie and Jase compete against each other to become president of their neighborhood homeowner association; Jep, Jess and Miss Kay work on a Duck Commander-themed display for the local zoo.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: The guys have fun with a 3-D printer until they come to the realization that the machine could soon replace their jobs; Si invests in John Luke's snow cone stand after Willie doesn't give him a raise.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Jase, Jep, Si and Cole battle a growing population of cottonmouth snakes on Phil's property; Willie uses his business expertise to help Rebecca improve her clothing store.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Little Will decides to buy a motorcycle instead of a car after Willie puts him on a budget; Jase and the guys get inspiration to design a revolutionary duck-hunting product involving zip lines.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: With many of the wives and children in Africa performing charity work, Willie and Jase have a debate over tracking ability; Jase and Si are challenged to track and capture Willie and Jep.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Willie and Jep step in to supervise Bella's sleepover while Korie is away in Africa; Jase takes Si, Martin and Godwin for a round of night golf.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Korie challenges Willie to come up with a romantic gesture without spending any money; Missy and Jase compete to see who can find a girlfriend for Mountain Man.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Jase's hopes to win the inaugural Duck Commander Fishing Tournament is buoyed when he is able to enlist the help of angler Bill Dance; Willie's vanity over his greying beard leads him to make a questionable decision.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Jase and Jep make a deal with Mia and Merritt to get them to go dove hunting; Si tries to make some extra money by starting a West Monroe tour.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Willie institutes a "no-tech week" when he feels his children spend too much time on their phones, but has difficulty participating in his own program; the guys attempt to stop a lice outbreak at Duck Commander.

programme.desc: Phil and Si make a mess out of career day at a local school; Jase and the boys conduct a nighttime search for frogs at Willie's country club.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: The boys blow up one of Phil's outdated duck blinds, then plan to build a new one in the sky; Miss Kay and the Robertson women sell the men's prized possessions in a yard sale.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Jase plans to build his own office when Willie refuses to give him a new one; a visit to a petting zoo brings out the farmer in Miss Kay.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Pesky beavers are too elusive for the Robertson boys to catch; Miss Kay takes over a local restaurant for an evening in an attempt to see if she can run her own establishment.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Willie and Jase drag their sons into a fishing competition; the Robertson ladies attempt to expand the company's women's line.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Willie plans to construct the world's largest duck call in celebration of the company's 40th anniversary; Miss Kay thinks it's time for a new home.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Jase and the guys take to Monroe's waters to battle an invasion of Asian carp; Willie and Si have to run Willie's Duck Diner with most of the staff out sick.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: The family relives old memories at its childhood summer camp and attempts to repair a tennis court Willie built 20 years earlier; while at the camp, Jep attempts to pass a lifeguard swimming test he failed as a teenager.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Rowdy officially becomes a Robertson after his adoption is finalized; Jase enjoys his first massage more than he expected.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.sub-title: Best of Duck Dynasty: Si Jobs
programme.desc: A compilation of the best scenes from all 11 seasons of the series.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.sub-title: Best of Duck Dynasty: It's All Duck and Games
programme.desc: A compilation of the best scenes from all 11 seasons of the series.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Willie becomes annoyed when Phil and Jase prefer to hunt and fish rather than attend to Duck Commander business; Willie helps Miss Kay with a cooking video.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Jase must take care of an important shipment after he is put in charge of the company for a day; Phil surprises his hardworking grandchildren.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Willie lands the biggest order in the company's history, prompting the boys to build a conveyor belt; Phil wants to blast a beaver dam to smithereens.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Willie and Jase drag their sons into a fishing competition; the Robertson ladies attempt to expand the company's women's line.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Willie plans to construct the world's largest duck call in celebration of the company's 40th anniversary; Miss Kay thinks it's time for a new home.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Willie, Jase and Uncle Si try to capture a beehive loaded with delicious honey; Korie and Jessica get into trouble while trying to find duck decoys for a carnival game.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: Willie wants to meet Sadie's new boyfriend in order to instill some healthy fatherly fear; Grandpa Phil and Uncle Si take Sadie on an outing.

programme.title: Odd Jobs
programme.desc: The summer job situation looks bleak for five college pals until they decide to start their own moving business.

programme.title: Pirate Radio
programme.desc: In 1966, BBC radio broadcasts less than an hour of pop music a day, forcing pirate DJs to take up the slack from boats anchored outside British waters. Quentin (Bill Nighy) is the commander of such a pirate station, overseeing a host of seedy, lusty and dope-smoking DJs, including the Count (Philip Seymour Hoffman) and Dave (Nick Frost), who makes it his personal mission to see to it that Quentin's newly arrived godson (Tom Sturridge) loses his virginity.

programme.title: The Lost Husband
programme.desc: A widow and her children move to her estranged aunt's farm in Texas.

programme.title: Waitress: The Musical
programme.desc: Jenna Hunterson is a waitress and expert pie maker stuck in a small town and a loveless marriage. When a baking contest offers her a chance at escape, Jenna fights to reclaim part of herself.

programme.title: Due Date
programme.desc: Peter Highman will be a dad for the first time when his wife gives birth in five days. He intends to catch a flight home from Atlanta so he can be there for the delivery, but a chance encounter with aspiring actor Ethan Tremblay throws a monkey wrench into his plans. Desperate to reach his wife before their baby is born, Peter's sanity is tested when he must take a road trip cross-country with dog-toting Ethan.

programme.title: Life as We Know It
programme.desc: After a disastrous first date, the only things Holly Berenson and Eric Messer have in common are mutual dislike and their love for their goddaughter, Sophie. When they unexpectedly become her caretakers, Holly and Eric have to put aside their differences and learn to work together for the sake of the child. Juggling competing career and social commitments, Holly and Eric look for common ground while living under the same roof.

programme.title: Sing Sing
programme.desc: A man is imprisoned at Sing Sing for a crime he didn't commit. When joined by a wary outsider, he finds purpose in staging an original comedy with a theater group of other incarcerated men.

programme.title: Nico, 1988
programme.desc: Approaching age 50, singer/songwriter Nico leads a solitary existence -- far from her days as a Warhol superstar and celebrated vocalist for the Velvet Underground in the 1960s. Her life and career on the fringes, Nico's new manager convinces her to hit the road again and tour in Europe to promote her latest album. Struggling with demons and the consequences of a muddled life, she longs to rebuild a relationship with her son, whose custody she lost long ago.

programme.title: Carol
programme.desc: Aspiring photographer Therese spots the beautiful, elegant Carol perusing the doll displays in a 1950s Manhattan department store. The two women develop a fast bond that becomes a love with complicated consequences.

programme.title: Supergirl
programme.desc: Kara (Helen Slater) of Argo City poses as Clark Kent's cousin, Linda Lee, to recover the Omegahedron from a witch (Faye Dunaway).

programme.title: Green Lantern
programme.desc: Sworn to preserve intergalactic order, the Green Lantern Corps has existed for centuries. Its newest recruit, Hal Jordan, is the first human to join the ranks. The Green Lanterns have little regard for humans, who have thus far been unable to harness the powers of the ring each member wears. But Jordan, a gifted and cocky test pilot, may be the corps' only hope when a new enemy called Parallax threatens the universal balance of power.

programme.title: Elevation
programme.desc: Post-apocalyptic survivors find refuge in the Rocky Mountains to hide from giant, insect-like creatures that can't live above 8,000 feet. However, when one of them needs life-saving supplies, they risk it all to venture into the danger zone.

programme.title: Split
programme.desc: Though Kevin has evidenced 23 personalities to his trusted psychiatrist, Dr. Fletcher, there remains one still submerged who is set to materialize and dominate all the others. Compelled to abduct three teenage girls led by the willful, observant Casey, Kevin reaches a war for survival among all of those contained within him -- as well as everyone around him -- as the walls between his compartments shatter.

programme.title: Heretic
programme.desc: A diabolical Mr. Reed ensnares two young missionaries in a terrifying game of cat and mouse when they knock on the wrong door and are forced to prove their faith.

programme.title: Heretic
programme.desc: A diabolical Mr. Reed ensnares two young missionaries in a terrifying game of cat and mouse when they knock on the wrong door and are forced to prove their faith.

programme.title: Miss You Already
programme.desc: A woman (Toni Collette) undergoes treatment to battle breast cancer while her best friend (Drew Barrymore) tries to get pregnant.

programme.title: Flashpoint
programme.desc: Texas border patrolmen (Kris Kristofferson, Treat Williams) find a jeep, a skeleton and $800,000 in cash dating from 1963.

programme.title: Raise Hell: The Life & Times of Molly Ivins
programme.desc: The life and work of Molly Ivins, a political journalist from Texas, best-selling author, and Bill of Rights warrior.

programme.title: Patti Cake$
programme.desc: Fed up with life in her New Jersey hometown, Patricia Dombrowski, aka Patti Cake$, hopes to follow in the footsteps of her idol and hit the road to achieve stardom as a rapper. With help from her best friend, a mysterious musician and her loving grandmother, Patti leads the charge against an army of haters, unpaid bills and the broken dreams that are holding her back.

programme.title: Wonka
programme.desc: Armed with nothing but a hatful of dreams, young chocolatier Willy Wonka manages to change the world, one delectable bite at a time.

programme.title: Red Riding Hood
programme.desc: Young Valerie (Amanda Seyfried) feels torn between two men. She loves Peter, but her parents intend that she marry wealthy Henry. Valerie and Peter are going to run away together, but her sister is killed by a werewolf before they can put their plan into action. The villagers call on the help of Father Solomon (Gary Oldman), a famous monster hunter who warns that the beast takes human form by day. Then Valerie suspects that the werewolf is someone she loves.

programme.title: A Haunted House
programme.desc: Young couple Malcolm (Marlon Wayans) and Kisha (Essence Atkins) have just moved into their dream home. As they settle in, they learn that they're not alone -- not because the house is haunted, but because Kisha is possessed by a demon. Desperate to salvage his relationship -- and more importantly -- his sex life -- Malcolm hires a coterie of paranormal specialists, which includes a priest and ghostbusters, to banish the unwelcome intruder from Kisha's body.

programme.title: A Haunted House 2
programme.desc: After the ordeal he suffered with his now former girlfriend's (Essence Atkins) demonic possession, Malcolm (Marlon Wayans) has decided to start fresh. He has a new girlfriend named Megan (Jaime Pressly), and they and Megan's children are moving into their dream home. Soon afterward, Malcolm is once again plagued by supernatural shenanigans -- only this time, it's not just the house that's haunted. A demon has attached itself to Malcolm, and shows no signs of letting go.

programme.title: Public Enemies
programme.desc: Depression-era bank robber John Dillinger's charm and audacity endear him to much of America's downtrodden public, but he's also a thorn in the side of J. Edgar Hoover and the fledgling FBI. Desperate to capture the elusive outlaw, Hoover makes Dillinger his first Public Enemy Number One and assigns his top agent, Melvin Purvis, the task of bringing him in dead or alive.

programme.title: Glass
programme.desc: David Dunn tries to stay one step ahead of the law while delivering vigilante justice on the streets of Philadelphia. His special talents soon put him on a collision course with the Beast -- the psychotic madman who has superhuman strength and 23 distinct personalities. Their epic showdown leads them to an encounter with the mysterious Elijah Price, the criminal mastermind who holds critical secrets for both men.

programme.title: Valerian and the City of a Thousand Planets
programme.desc: In the 28th century, special operatives Valerian and Laureline work together to maintain order throughout the human territories. Under assignment from the minister of defense, the duo embarks on a mission to Alpha, an ever-expanding metropolis where diverse species gather to share knowledge and culture. When a dark force threatens the peaceful city, Valerian and Laureline must race against time to identify the menace that also jeopardizes the future of the universe.

programme.title: Evil Dead Rise
programme.desc: A reunion between two estranged sisters gets cut short by the rise of flesh-possessing demons, thrusting them into a primal battle for survival as they face the most nightmarish version of family imaginable.

programme.title: Woodshock
programme.desc: A haunted young woman spirals into confusion and chaos after she falls under the spell of a potent, reality-altering drug.

programme.title: Tiny House Nation
programme.desc: A Little Rock couple builds a 264 sq. foot mobile honeymoon pad as they prepare to get married and hit the road as traveling nurses.

programme.title: Tiny House Nation
programme.desc: John and Zack have to design a 172 sq. foot space that includes a pink castle, full kitchen, and master bedroom with privacy.

programme.title: Tiny House Nation
programme.desc: A Florida veteran working two full-time jobs to support his family hopes that going tiny will lower the bills so he can work less and spend more time with his children; despite going tiny, the family's wish list is huge, and includes a swimming pool.

programme.title: Tiny House Nation
programme.desc: Anna and Aaron are wedding photographers who have decided to go tiny in order to move their Denver business to warmer climates to maximize clientele year round.

programme.title: Tiny House Nation
programme.desc: Tommy and Katie are world-class triathletes who ask John and Zack to help create their first home and make it a space that gives them both a cozy place to come back to but can still function as a space to train in between competitions.

programme.title: Tiny House Nation
programme.sub-title: 480 Sq. Ft. The Gothic Castle House
programme.desc: While Cally and Steve love horror films and gothic style, daughter Miura loves pastels and pearls; John and Zack must build a tiny house that fits their differing tastes.

programme.title: Tiny House Nation
programme.desc: John and Zack head to California's Sonoma County to help volunteer firefighter Scott and his wife, Xanthie, build a home to replace the one they lost in the Northern California wildfires of 2017.

programme.title: Tiny House Hunting
programme.desc: Drew Smith, a Navy doctor, and his wife, Libby, look for a 700-square-foot craftsman home in the historic streetcar neighborhood of North Park.

programme.title: Tiny House Hunting
programme.sub-title: Tiny Getaway Retreat in Kona, Hawaii
programme.desc: Jenn and her boyfriend are hunting for a tiny retreat on the Big Island of Hawaii; they know the perfect tiny getaway in Kona, a place they both love, will give them somewhere to relax and reconnect.

programme.title: Tiny House Hunting
programme.sub-title: A New Home for Newlyweds in Portland
programme.desc: Jake and Emmy are in the market for a tiny home in their beloved Portland where they can truly begin their married lives.

programme.title: Tiny House Hunting
programme.desc: Nikki and Tomislav are ready to reestablish their roots and agree that going tiny will allow them to live downtown and explore the city.

programme.title: Tiny House Hunting
programme.desc: Jeff has decided to hunt for a tiny getaway in Honolulu, where he loves the laid-back lifestyle and already has a community of friends.

programme.title: Tiny House Hunting
programme.desc: Kathlyn and her husband Lincoln saved enough money to buy their dream house, but it turns out that they actually miss living on top of each other.

programme.title: Tiny House Hunting
programme.sub-title: Tiny Honeymoon Home in San Diego
programme.desc: Bianca and Sara are house hunting and planning their wedding, at the same time. To stay in one of the trendy and pricey San Diego neighborhoods they love, they're going tiny.

programme.title: Tiny House Hunting
programme.sub-title: Tiny For a Family of Five in Temecula
programme.desc: With three teenagers in the house, Joe and Sheri live a hectic life of sports, homework and family meals, but they'll be empty nesters soon, and it's motivating them to look tiny.

programme.title: Tiny House Hunting
programme.desc: Eric and Anna are two Generation Y-ers in Seattle, Washington and they're ready to take the tiny plunge.

programme.title: Tiny House Hunting
programme.sub-title: What Started an Ocean Away is Now Tiny Today
programme.desc: Cody and Karen met in Hawaii and have found themselves looking to go tiny in San Francisco with an outdoor space for their two dogs.

programme.title: Tiny House Hunting
programme.desc: Anthony and Angela miss the hustle and bustle of their overseas lives, and they hope to find the vibrant equivalent in the heart of Beantown.

programme.title: Tiny House Hunting
programme.desc: A proud resident of the Pacific Northwest thinks he's got what it takes to live in 300 square feet or less, but doesn't want to bite off more tiny than he can chew.

programme.title: Tiny House Hunting
programme.desc: Chris and Maggie are ready to try something they've never done before, live with each other.

programme.title: Tiny House Nation
programme.sub-title: 340 Sq. Ft. Big Dogs, Tiny House
programme.desc: Sean and Kate have decided to travel the country and have asked John and Zack to help them create a modern home that sleeps everyone comfortably, including their big furry friends, and is durable enough to take on some miles.

programme.title: Tiny House Nation
programme.desc: John and Zack take on a rescue mission in Atlanta for Peter and Elizabeth, a couple who are struggling to finish construction on their own tiny house, are about to get married, and plan to change jobs and move to Maine all in a month's time.

programme.title: Tiny House Nation
programme.desc: CJ and Christina lead successful but stressful professional lives out in Silicon Valley while also raising their boys, JJ and baby Max. They're looking to escape the tech in the valley and retreat to what they hope will be a tiny sanctuary.

programme.title: Tiny House Nation
programme.desc: John and Zack are tasked with creating a place that helps Asha, a professional violinist, and Ryan, an adventure journalist, build a stronger bond while also inspiring them to continue to grow creatively.

programme.title: Tiny House Nation
programme.desc: Josh and Shelley bring in John and Zack to build them a transformative tiny house with an indoor/outdoor entertaining space that the couple can use all year long.

programme.title: Tiny House Nation
programme.desc: Phillise and her boys are committed to living off grid; the house is passively designed to take advantage of the California sun.

programme.title: Tiny House Nation
programme.desc: Michelle and Blake love having their friends over for drinks and some card playing; they've asked John and Zack to help build them a tiny house that not only has space to entertain their friends, but room to keep their two dogs entertained as well.
